title: Xeraea virgata
intro: Xeraea virgata, also known as Puna Bursera or Puna Tree, is a shrub or small tree native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Xeraea virgata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with Xeraea virgata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and promote relaxation.
